mnemonics: Diverse Ensemble Creates Better Models: Remember Decision (D) by Committee's core idea that an ensemble (E) of diverse (D) models (committee members) creates (C) a better (B) final model (M).
keyPoints: It's an ensemble method that combines predictions from multiple models (the 'committee') to improve accuracy and reduce overfitting.
It works by aggregating the predictions, often through a majority vote (for classification) or averaging (for regression).
Its effectiveness relies on the committee members being diverse, meaning they make different errors on different data points.
commonMistakes: Assuming all ensemble members must be of the same algorithm type (e.g., all decision trees). Bagging often does this, but a committee can be made of fundamentally different models (e.g., an SVM, a neural net, and a logistic regression).
Confusing it with Boosting. Boosting trains models sequentially, where each new model corrects its predecessor. Committee methods typically train models in parallel.
